twitter_thread: I purged 17 kitchen gadgets and now cook with just 5 essentials.
Ditched my $10 silicone egg molds for a $5 metal ring mold.
A $2 microplane beats a $20 garlic press any day.
The $40 Spiralizer? Just use a $5 julienne peeler instead.
A $10 thermometer has improved my cooking more than any other gadget.
Single-use gadgets like pineapple corers are 100% waste.
What's the most expensive kitchen gadget you've regretted buying? #kitchentools #cooking
pack.linkedin: After cutting my kitchen gadget collection from 22 to 5, I've found that function beats novelty every time. A metal ring mold replaces silicone egg molds, and a chef's knife with a microplane outperforms a garlic press. With a few strategic tools, I've saved $200 and gained more counter space. What's the one kitchen gadget you couldn't imagine cooking without, and is there one that you wish you'd never bought?
tiktok_hooks: I just got rid of 17 kitchen gadgets and I'm cooking better than ever
These 5 kitchen tools are the only ones you'll ever need
Don't waste your money on these useless kitchen gadgets
pack.reddit_headline: I replaced 17 kitchen gadgets with just 5 essentials, what's your most regretful kitchen purchase?
